question: Is the koi's base body color predominantly pale/white (utilizing negative space) with ink shading, rather than being fully red/orange like the structure reference?
Does the koi feature a distinct orange/cinnabar patch specifically on the top of its head, matching the subject reference's pattern?
Are black spots present and scattered along the dorsal area (back) of the fish, consistent with the subject reference?
Is the image rendered in a traditional freehand ink wash (sumi-e) style with visible ink gradations used to convey the fish's volume?
Do the edges of the fish and elements exhibit 'feibai' (flying white/dry brush) texture, avoiding sharp digital or photorealistic hard edges?
Are the orange markings rendered as restrained 'cinnabar' ink touches rather than opaque, saturated, or photorealistic coloring?
Is the background primarily negative space (white/paper) with only very light ink washes to suggest water, avoiding realistic water ripples?
Does the fish strictly follow the C-shaped swimming arc pose defined in the structure reference image?
Are there exactly two lotus leaves positioned at the top and bottom of the composition, mirroring the structure reference layout?
Is there an ink splatter or splash effect present on the right side of the composition, as seen in the structure reference?
Is the fish free from obvious anatomical distortions, such as malformed fins, disconnected tail, or unnatural proportions?
Does the image avoid muddy rendering and digital artifacts, maintaining a clean and cohesive ink wash look?
